LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS AIRA DE VERA. AND AISLINN BAUTISTA'S MOTION TO ENTER JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. TRANSFERRED TO BE HEARD IN DEPARTMENT 501 ON AUGUST 8, 2025, AT 9:30 A.M. THAT DEPARTMENT HANDLES REAL PROPERTY DISPUTES PER SF LOCAL RULE 8.10A1. MOVANT TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.7B AND DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR RESPECTIVE FILINGS IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION TO DEPARTMENT 501 WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE AND DEPARTMENT NO LATER THAN JULY 21, 2025. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO ENTER JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THERE ARE SEVERAL ISSUES WITH THE MOVING PAPERS. THE PROOF OF SERVICE IS DATED JUNE 12, 2024, ATTESTING TO SERVICE ON JUNE 12, 2025. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT HAVE COMPETENT EVIDENCE OF A BREACH BECAUSE HE DOES NOT EXPLAIN HOW HE HAS PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE THAT DEFENDANT "DID NOT PAY" PLAINTIFFS. COUNSEL PROVIDES NO DATES OR EXHIBITS OF HIS ATTEMPTS TO CONTACT OR LOCATE OPPOSING COUNSEL, NO EXPLANATION FOR WHY PLAINTIFF WAITED 9 MONTHS AFTER THE PURPORTED BREACH TO BRING THIS MOTION, AND ALSO APPEARS TO HAVE SIGNED THIS AGREEMENT APPROVING IT AS TO FORM 10 DAYS AFTER THE FIRST PAYMENT WAS DUE. FINALLY, THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT SEEMS TO CONTRADICT ITSELF BECAUSE IT REQUIRES AN INITIAL PAYMENT OF $7,500 "TO EACH PLAINTIFF," WHICH WOULD SATISFY THE ENTIRE SETTLEMENT AMOUNT, FOLLOWED BY ADDITIONAL MONTHLY PAYMENTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-24-2026 Set For Case Management Conference On Oct-28-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enter Judgment Pursuant To Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Aug-08-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO ENTER JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. There are several issues with the moving papers. The Proof of Service is dated June 12, 2024, attesting to service on June 12, 2025. Plaintiff does not have competent evidence of a breach because he does not explain how he has personal knowledge that Defendant "did not pay" plaintiffs. Counsel provides no dates or exhibits of his attempts to contact or locate opposing counsel, no explanation for why Plaintiff waited 9 months after the purported breach to bring this motion, and also appears to have signed this agreement approving it as to form 10 days after the first payment was due. Finally, the settlement agreement seems to contradict itself because it requires an initial payment of $7,500 "to each Plaintiff," which would satisfy the entire settlement amount, followed by additional monthly payments. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enter Judgment Pursuant To Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jul-17-2025 Continued To Aug-08-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Richard Michael Lieberman, Esq. 415-929-3197 for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

ARGUED. Mr. Lieberman argues the case is a personal injury matter and not a real property matter, The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs AIRA DE VERA. and AISLINN BAUTISTA's Motion To Enter Judgment Pursuant To Settlement Agreement.

Transferred to be heard in department 501 on August 8, 2025, at 9:30 a.m. That department handles real property disputes per SF Local Rule 8.10A1. Movant to comply with SFLR 2.7B and deliver courtesy copies of their respective filings in support of the motion to department 501 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date and department no later than July 21, 2025.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
